body: r_c can't help but feel that the Clan is better off without m_c's lies.
r_c breathes a sigh of relief now that one of the Clan’s most untrustworthy cats is gone.
r_c is relieved that {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} no longer {VERB/r_c/have/has} to worry about m_c’s lies, betrayal, or deceit again now that {PRONOUN/m_c/subject}{VERB/m_c/'re/'s} gone.
r_c gazes over m_c’s body in the clearing during the vigil and lets out a sigh of relief. Nobody here will be deceived by this cat ever again.
r_c finds {PRONOUN/r_c/self} lingering by m_c’s body, wanting to make sure that {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} {VERB/m_c/aren’t/isn’t} just playing dead. r_c wouldn’t put it past {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.

body: Though {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} may not have trusted m_c as far as {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} could throw {PRONOUN/m_c/object}, r_c realizes that not every cat holds the same opinion. With that in mind, r_c spends the days following m_c's death comforting those who seem particularly affected.
